Investigating 'life' via

Principal Investigator
Sahutchai Inwongwan · DPhil Oxford

Sahutchai Inwongwan

Scientist · Biologist · Biochemist
Lecturer in Systems Biology, Dept. of Biology, Chiang Mai University, Thailand
Honorary Research Fellow, GEE, University College London, UK
DPhil, Dept. of Plant Sciences, University of Oxford, UK

Sahutchai's DPhil at Oxford explored the capacities of the Euglena metabolic network using metabolic flux analysis. He subsequently held a research fellowship at University College London studying mitonuclear incompatibility in Drosophila, before returning to Chiang Mai University to establish the Systems Integration Laboratory.

Research

Research Areas

Our lab operates at the intersection of experimental biology and computational modelling, tackling fundamental and applied questions across six interconnected themes.

🌿
Euglena gracilis — Metabolic Modelling & Biorefinery
We apply genome-scale metabolic modelling (GSMM) and metabolic flux analysis to map and optimise the central metabolic network of Euglena gracilis — a uniquely versatile microalga capable of photoautotrophic, heterotrophic, and mixotrophic growth. Our work spans carbon capture, beta-glucan and paramylon production, shikimate-derived natural product synthesis, and the development of Euglena as an industrial biofactory chassis.
Funded by: National Research Council of Thailand · Murata Science Foundation · Chiang Mai University
⚡
Organelle Bioenergetics & Mitonuclear Interactions
We investigate the functional coordination between mitochondria and chloroplasts in Euglena gracilis, exploring how these two energy-converting organelles integrate their metabolic fluxes during photosynthesis and respiration. In parallel, we study mitonuclear incompatibility in Drosophila — how mismatches between mitochondrial and nuclear genomes affect energy metabolism, nutritional preference, and fitness.
In collaboration with: Prof. Nick Lane (UCL) · Prof. M. Florencia Camus (UCL) · BBSRC (UK)
🌫️
PM2.5 & Lung Cancer — Multi-omics Analysis
Northern Thailand experiences severe seasonal biomass-burning haze events. We use transcriptomics, metabolomics, and proteomics to understand how locally-derived PM2.5 particles rewire the metabolism of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) cells — driving oxidative stress, altering gene expression, and contributing to therapy resistance. Our goal is to connect environmental exposure to molecular cancer biology.
Funded by: National Research Council of Thailand (Fundamental Fund)
🚀
Astrobiology & Astrobioinnovation
We model the metabolic limits of life under extreme and extraterrestrial conditions — from prebiotic chemistry to planetary surfaces. Using genome-scale metabolic modelling of extremophiles, we predict how organisms might survive low-oxygen, high-radiation, or nutrient-scarce environments. We also investigate Euglena gracilis as a candidate for bioregenerative life-support systems (BLSS) in space, characterising its UVC stress responses and physiological flexibility under space-analog stressors.
Affiliated with: Research Group on Space Weather & Cosmic Rays, CMU · Environmental Microbiology Reports (Wiley Top Viewed Article 2025)
🍯
Fermentation Science & Beverage Biochemistry
We study the physicochemical and biological transformations that occur during fermentation of honey-based beverages (mead), with a focus on honeys sourced from the Mekong region. Our research characterises changes in bioactive compounds, antioxidant activity, and microbial community dynamics across fermentation. We aim to develop optimised fermentation strategies and microbial tools for producing high-value functional beverages.
Local collaboration: Dept. of Biology, CMU · Regional honey producers, Northern Thailand
🐝
Honeybee Microbiome & Pollinator Health
The gut microbiome of honeybees plays a critical role in pollinator health, nutrition, and immunity. We use genome-scale metabolic modelling and multi-omics approaches to map the metabolic potential of key gut symbionts, identify beneficial microbial interactions, and explore how microbiome engineering can support bee growth and resilience. We also study fungal microbiome diversity in stingless bee species native to Thailand.
In collaboration with: Assoc. Prof. Terd Disayathanoowat (CMU) · Dr. Nuttapol Noirungsee (CMU)

News & Recognition

Lab News

🏆 Recognition · Wiley 2025

Top Viewed Article — Environmental Microbiology Reports

Our review on genome-scale metabolic modelling of extremophiles has been recognised as a top-viewed article in Environmental Microbiology Reports among work published January–December 2024. The paper explores how GSMM can illuminate life under prebiotic environments and space-related conditions — from extremophile metabolism to potential astrobiology applications.

We are grateful for the wide readership and engagement from the scientific community. The field is moving forward quickly, but we hope the core ideas and frameworks remain useful.

Read the paper → doi:10.1111/1758-2229.13231
📄
New Publication · March 2026

Trophic Condition Shapes UVC Responses in Euglena gracilis

Published in Life (MDPI, 2026). Mixotrophic cultivation supported by ethanol shows improved physiological robustness under repeated UVC stress compared to photoautotrophy — with implications for bioregenerative life-support and space biology.

Read the paper → doi:10.3390/life16040539
Expertise

Key Skills

Spanning wet-lab experimentation, computational modelling, and advanced analytics.

🔬
Systems Biology & Multi-Omics
Multi-modal integration of transcriptomic, metabolomic, and proteomic datasets for network reconstruction.
🧮
Metabolic Engineering & Fluxomics
Genome-Scale Metabolic Modeling (GSMM), COBRA toolbox, Metabolic Flux Analysis (MFA).
⚗️
Analytical Chemistry (GC-MS/LC-MS)
High-precision metabolite profiling, biomass characterization, and natural product identification.
🦠
Algal & Microbial Biotechnology
Photoautotrophic and mixotrophic cultivation optimisation for Euglena gracilis.
🧬
Molecular Biology
NGS library preparation, RNA-seq, qPCR, and advanced cloning strategies.
🔭
Advanced Bio-Imaging
Confocal, fluorescence, and electron microscopy (SEM/TEM); FACS cytometry.
⚡
Organelle Bioenergetics
Mitonuclear coordination analysis and photosynthesis–respiration trade-off quantification.
🏭
Bioprocess Development
Scale-up and downstream processing for algal biomass and fermentation products.
